trivia_bullets.text: War of the Spark (WAR) was released May 3, 2019 and depicted Nicol Bolas's invasion of Ravnica. The Gruul Clans — whose cyclops forces lived in the Rubblebelt on the city's outskirts — were drawn into the chaos. Domri Rade, the Gruul Guildmaster, aligned with Bolas and was the first named planeswalker to have his spark harvested by the Elderspell.
sources: MTG Wiki: War of the Spark/Event via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: 'You say this Tenth District, not Rubblebelt. But where smash happen, that Rubblebelt. Rubblebelt state of mind.' — Urgdar, cyclops philosopher. The Rubblebelt is canonically the Gruul-controlled zone of destroyed buildings on Ravnica's outskirts; Bolas's invasion turned much of the Tenth District into de facto Rubblebelt. Urgdar's philosophical position is the card's lone comedy note in an otherwise grim set.
sources: MTG Wiki: Gruul Clans via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: A {4}{R} 4/4 with {3}{R}: target creature can't block this turn. EDHREC tracks the card in approximately 240 decks (0.0% of all), ranking it as one of the lower-inclusion WAR commons in Commander. Standard relevance was minimal given its cost and the competitive environment of WAR Standard.
sources: EDHREC: Chainwhip Cyclops via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Chainwhip Cyclops was reprinted in Ravnica: Clue Edition (CLU-127, 2024), a crossover product themed around the Ravnica setting and the Clue board game. The reprint recontextualizes the card inside Ravnica's city geography.
